body.title: CLOSED: ADC MCP tool: file_ticket — create cc_queue ticket from any device
body.result: file_ticket() MCP tool writing to clan.memories; 4 tests
body.description:
No MCP tool exists to file a ticket from the Librarian or any ADC device. Creating tickets requires CC or an indirect channel message. This blocks the three-way conspiracy loop (Librarian notices issue → files ticket → CC sprints).

Proposed shape: Add `file_ticket(title, description, size='S', tags=None, decision_id=None, priority=0.5, status='triage')` to Librarian tools. Writes directly to `clan.memories` using cc_queue's schema: id=T-<slug>, narrative=title, memory_type='FACTUAL', parent_id='TICKETS_ROOT', metadata={kind:'ticket', id, title, description, size, tags, status, priority, decision_id}, source='cc_queue', scope='class', confidence=1.0. No TheIgors import — the schema is known from inspection of cc_queue.py INSERT. Logs to action_log after insert.

**Affected files:** new `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/ticket_tools.py`, update `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/__init__.py`
**Design rules:** no-TheIgors-imports; no-sqlite; action-log-every-call
**Scope boundary:** file_ticket only. No list_tickets, no status updates — those later. Status always 'triage' unless caller specifies.
**Test plan:** call file_ticket, verify row in clan.memories with parent_id=TICKETS_ROOT, kind='ticket'; verify action_log entry; verify ticket appears in cc_queue list output.
